sections_cited: Section 24 of the Pakistan Penal Code

chunks.text: Form No: HCJD/C-121 ORDER SHEET IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, LAHORE (JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT) Case No. Writ Petition No.67828/2022 Azhar Nawaz Versus Addl. Sessions Judge, etc. Sr.No.of order/ Proceedings Date of order/ Proceedings Order with signatures of Judge, and that of parties or counsel, where necessary. 13.12.2022 Mr. Farhan Tawakal, Advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Muhammad Iqbal Khan, Advocate for respondent No.3. Mr. Shamshad Ullah Malhi, A.A.G. with Arif,ASI. ====== Through this petition under Article 199 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, petitioner Azhar Nawaz has challenged the legality and propriety of order dated 26.10.2022 passed by the learned Ex-Officio Justice of Peace, Gujranwala whereby upon the application of respondent No.3 (hereinafter shall be called as respondent) filed under Section 22-A Cr.P.C. a direction has been issued for registration of case against the petitioner. 2. Facts necessary for disposal of instant writ petition are that respondent filed an application under Sections 22-A & 22-B Cr.P.C. seeking registration of criminal case against the petitioner alleging therein that he in order to clear his fiscal liability, issued cheque No.1710123739 dated 20.10.2021 for an amount of Rs.18,90,000/-, which was dishonoured on its presentation before the concerned bank. Respondent in first instance filed an application for registration of case before the concerned SHO but no action was taken thereon, which constrained him to file application under Writ Petition No.67828/2022 2 Section 22-A & 22-B Cr.P.C. before the learned Ex-Officio Justice of Peace. 3. Learned Ex Officio Justice of Peace, after obtaining comments from the local police, vide impugned order dated 26.10.2022 directed the SHO concerned to record the statement of respondent U/S 154 Cr.P.C. and then proceed strictly in accordance with law, which is subject matter of instant writ petition. 4.

Respondent in first instance filed an application for registration of case before the concerned SHO but no action was taken thereon, which constrained him to file application under Writ Petition No.67828/2022 2 Section 22-A & 22-B Cr.P.C. before the learned Ex-Officio Justice of Peace. 3. Learned Ex Officio Justice of Peace, after obtaining comments from the local police, vide impugned order dated 26.10.2022 directed the SHO concerned to record the statement of respondent U/S 154 Cr.P.C. and then proceed strictly in accordance with law, which is subject matter of instant writ petition. 4. Heard. It is straightway observed that respondent intends to get register a criminal case against the petitioner on the allegation that he in order to clear his fiscal liability, dishonestly issued cheque No.1710123739 for an amount of Rs.18,90,000/- in his favour, which was dishonoured on its presentation before the concerned bank. I have gone through the photo- copy of disputed cheque and observed that the said cheque was not issued in favour of the respondent rather in the column of Pay word “self’ was find mentioned. Moot point which requires determination is that as to whether a cheque issued by an account holder to “himself” can create any criminal liability. Before proceeding further it is appropriate to go through the provisions of Section 489-F of PPC, which reads as under:- 489-F. Dishonestly issuing a cheque.—Whoever dishonestly issues a cheque towards payment of a loan or fulfillment of an obligation which is dishonoured on presentation, shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to three years or with fine, or with both, unless he can establish, for which the burden of proof shall rest on him, that he had made arrangements with his bank to ensure that the cheque would be honoured and that bank was at fault in not honouring the cheque.” Writ Petition No.67828/2022 3 While going through the above section, one can easily draw the conclusion that foundational element to constitute an offence under this provision is issuance of cheque with dishonest intent, the cheque should be towards repayment of loan or fulfillment of an obligation and lastly that the cheque in question is dishonoured.

Before proceeding further it is appropriate to go through the provisions of Section 489-F of PPC, which reads as under:- 489-F. Dishonestly issuing a cheque.—Whoever dishonestly issues a cheque towards payment of a loan or fulfillment of an obligation which is dishonoured on presentation, shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to three years or with fine, or with both, unless he can establish, for which the burden of proof shall rest on him, that he had made arrangements with his bank to ensure that the cheque would be honoured and that bank was at fault in not honouring the cheque.” Writ Petition No.67828/2022 3 While going through the above section, one can easily draw the conclusion that foundational element to constitute an offence under this provision is issuance of cheque with dishonest intent, the cheque should be towards repayment of loan or fulfillment of an obligation and lastly that the cheque in question is dishonoured. Section 24 of the Pakistan Penal Code, 1860 defines term ‘dishonestly’ as under:- “ Whoever does anything with the intention of causing wrongful gain to one person or wrongful loss to another person is said to do that thing “dishonestly.” It is thus quite evident that in order to attract the provision of Section 489-F PPC, intention of the account holder to cause wrongful gain to one person or wrongful loss to another was sina qua non. In case where there was a “self cheque” it can easily be presumed that the amount for which the cheque was issued was to be paid to the drawer himself and obviously the drawer would not dishonestly issue cheque to himself and the said cheque in any eventuality cannot be presumed to be issued towards repayment of loan or fulfillment of any obligation to oneself. For clarity, it is held that if in column of “pay” of any cheque, the word “self” “cash”, “in person” is written or left blank then offence under Section 489-F PPC is not made out. 5. Now the question arises that if the cheque is issued to “Self” but the same was handed over to someone else for collection of funds and upon its dishonor if such person approaches the police for registration of case then what would be its fate. Writ Petition No.67828/2022 4 The answer is quite simple and straightforward. In that eventuality, it would only be considered a bearer cheque open for encashment by anyone to whom the drawer does not owe or might not intended to pay anything. 6. Learned Ex Officio Justice of Peace although took note of this fact but proceeded to pass impugned direction on the basis of agreement 20.04.2021.

In that eventuality, it would only be considered a bearer cheque open for encashment by anyone to whom the drawer does not owe or might not intended to pay anything. 6. Learned Ex Officio Justice of Peace although took note of this fact but proceeded to pass impugned direction on the basis of agreement 20.04.2021. No doubt in the said agreement number of disputed cheque was find mentioned but the same was issued as security. Therefore, even if the contents of agreement are taken as gossipal truth even then no case of criminal liability is made out against the petitioner. Learned Ex Officio Justice of Peace has failed to comprehend correct facts of the case and passed the impugned order in summarily manner which cannot be allowed to hold the field. 7. For what has been discussed above, instant writ petition is allowed as a result whereof impugned order dated 26.10.2022 is hereby set- aside. (Asjad Javaid Ghural) Judge Approved for Reporting Judge Azam*
